text: President Dave Zimmerman called the meeting to order at 7:30 p.m. and the following members
were present: Aaron Whitwer, Angela Meyer, Betsy Frerichs, Dana Dorn, Dave Zimmerman,
and Jim Zvolanek. The following administrators were presents: Jerry Rempe, Jeff Murphy,
Reasonable advance publicized notice of the meeting was given according to law by publishing,
a designated method for giving notice of the school district. Posted Location:
Reasonable advance notice was simultaneously given to board members and a copy of their
acknowledgement of receipt of notice and the agenda attached. All proceedings hereafter shown
were taken while the convened meeting was open to the attendance of the public.
I.B. Notice of Nebraska Open Meetings Act Posted
President Dave Zimmerman announced that a complete copy of the Nebraska Open Meetings
Act is posted in the auditorium.
II. Approval of Minutes from the May 10, 2021, Regular Board Meeting
Motion to approve minutes from the May 10, 2021, Regular Board Meeting. This motion, made
by Dana Dorn and seconded by Jim Zvolanek, passed.
III.A. Public comments will not be received after this period of time. Public comment period is
limited to 5 minutes per person and a total of 30 minutes overall.
IV. Financial Statement: Item for Discussion, Consideration, and/or Action
C.D. Bids for the month were as follows:
Security First Bank, Blue Springs Special Funds: 0.18%
Dr. Prososki noted that the district is 9 months into the fiscal year and the district has received
92% of its revenue and expended 69% of its budget. Dr. Prososki noted that schools in Nebraska
do not receive state aid in the months of July and August, so the districts overall revenue will be
IV.A.1. General Fund, Special Building Fund, Depreciation Fund, and Qualified Capital Purpose
Motion to approve the general fund, special building fund, depreciation fund, and qualified
capitol purpose undertaking fund claims. This motion, made by Dana Dorn and seconded by
Betsy Frerichs: Absent, Aaron Whitwer: Abstain (With Conflict), Dana Dorn: yes, Angela
Meyer: yes, David Zimmerman: yes, Jim Zvolanek: yes
John Linder provided the school board with a written report over carpeting 4 rooms at the
Elementary School and 3 rooms at the Jr./Sr. High School, Con Pro starting on the concrete and
drainage project at the football field, installing new bleachers at the football field and getting rid
of the old 5 portable bleachers that date back to 1960s, and Johnson Controls starting on the
auditorium HVAC project and Jr./Sr. High School boiler project.
Dr. Prososki noted that the auditorium HVAC project and Jr./Sr. High School boiler project will
be funded with the second and third rounds of Elementary Secondary School Emergency Relief
Dr. Prososki has been working on receiving a new funding source from the federal government
called Emergency Connectivity Fund (EFC). Based on the district's strategic plan for the
acquisition of technology to support student learning, the district will be purchasing new teacher
laptops this fall. Based on the EFC funding, the district will receive around $16,000 to pay for
half of the cost of the teacher laptops. Dr. Prososki noted that there are a variety of steps the
district will have to take to receive this federal funding (Keep track of staff names on each
device, keep the devices for a number of years, survey teachers regarding devices at home, etc.).
Lastly, Dr. Prososki gave the board an update on the Safe Schools trainings for the 2021-2022
The elementary principal reported on the following items: having around 20 students in
kindergarten in the fall, 32 students attending summer school through the month of June,
teachers attending ESU 5 trainings, printing calendars for the 2021-2022 school year, and
summer weight room during the month of June and July.
The secondary principal reported on the following items: 3 of the 4 alternative education spots
will be full in August, student council elections (Carley Saathoff will be president and Jentry
Schell will be vice-president), Southern had 4 players make the Southeast Nebraska All-Star
Basketball and Volleyball Teams (Carson Borzekofski, Preston Baehr, Kaylee Klover, & Tori
Smith), and he gave the school board an update on honor roll percentages.
Dr. Prososki gave the school board an update on the third round of stimulus funding from the
American Rescue Plan and the district will receive $689,666. As of now and based on the
allowable areas where this funding can be spent towards, the district plans to use the third round
of stimulus funding to address deferred maintenance projects in both buildings (Boilers and
HVAC), purchase new textbooks (Science, Social Studies, Elementary School Reading
intervention program, and social and emotional curriculum [PK-8]), and purchase new
iPads/laptops to support the district's one-to-one initiative in grades PK-12 (The district will also
solicit feedback from the public regarding this proposed plan, which is a requirement from the
federal government). Next, Dr. Prososki gave the board an update on a draft of the public bond
election presentation, and he noted that he does not foresee students being required to get the
COVID-19 vaccine before they can return to school in the fall. Dr. Prososki went over a
preliminary budget presentation with the school board and noted the following items: (1) the
district's valuation has dropped 3 out of the last 6 years, (2) the district's mill levy has dropped by
25 cents since the 2011-2012 school year, (3) the district's mill levy has not increased in 4 years
(If the bond passes, the mill levy will go from $1.08 to $1.125), (4) the district's property tax
request has dropped 4 out of the last 5 years, (5) during the 2019-2020 school year, Southern's
cost per pupil (student) ranked the 3rd lowest in the Pioneer Conference, and it was lower than
over half the schools in Nebraska, (6) Dr. Prososki noted that he has only been able to grow
Southern's budget by $60,020 or by 1.12% each year over a 5-year period and the previous
superintendent was able to grow his budget by $167,803 or by 3.54% each year over a 5-year
period. Dr. Prososki then noted he will serve as both the MUDECAS and Pioneer Conference
presidents during the 2021-2022 school year, he presented the school board with the final
Nebraska legislative report (107th Legislative, 1st Session), he gave the school board an update
on teacher negotiations, he predicted the district valuation will increase by $2-3 million dollar
this coming school year, and he gave an update on the districts strategic plan (Principle II: Whole
Child Focus) to address social and emotional learning in an effort to provide a supportive
learning environment that forms a cohesive foundation for student success. Dr. Prososki went
over the finalized state aid numbers for 2021-2022 (The district's state aid will go up by a meager
$23,589 or by 1.56%), noted that he has submitted a SPED IDEA Consolidated grant and a
Support Service Flex Funding School Age grant and the district should receive around $126,000
from both grants. Lastly, Dr. Prososki wrote 2 grants to the Thomas Foundation for new visitor
bleachers and for new equipment for the industrial arts and science areas.
VII. Items for Discussion, Consideration, and/or Action
VII.A. DA Davidson Special Bond Election Financial Information
Andy Forney from DA Davidson went over the financial aspects of the September 14, 2021,
special bond election, and he went over sample ballot language that the school board will
approve at the July board meeting.
Review, consider, and take all necessary action to adopt a resolution (1) authorizing the
publication in a newspaper of general circulation within the District of a request for proposals for
a design-builder; (2) designating members of the Design-Build Selection Committee; (3)
referring all design-build proposals to the Design-Build Selection Committee and (4) taking any
other action deemed necessary regarding the selection of the design-build contract or required by
the Political Subdivisions Construction Alternatives Act. This motion, made by Angela Meyer
and seconded by Betsy Frerichs, passed.
VII.C. Set Breakfast, Lunch, & Milk Prices for the 2021-2022 School Year
Motion to raise the breakfast & lunch prices by $0.05 for the 2021-2022 school year. This
motion, made by Dana Dorn and seconded by Betsy Frerichs, passed.
Dr. Prososki noted that the United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) announced in May
that all school breakfasts and lunches will be free during the 2021-2022 school year. Dr. Prososki
noted that he wants school breakfast and school lunch prices to keep up with inflation and he
wants to increase them gradually overtime to be in compliance with the required paid lunch
For the 2021-2022 school year, the breakfast prices in grades PK-12 will be $2.15, lunch prices
in grades PK-6 will be $3.05, lunch prices in grades 7-12 will be $3.15, adult breakfast prices
will be $2.35, and adult lunches will be $3.80.
VII.D. Approval of Policy 5045: Student Fees
Motion to approve Policy 5045: Student Fees. This motion, made by Angela Meyer and
VII.E. Approval of Policy 5018 & 5057: Parent and Guardian Involvement in Education
Practices & District Title I Parent and Family Engagement Policy
Motion to approve Policy 5018 & 5057: Parent and Guardian Involvement in Education
Practices & District Title I Parent and Family Engagement Policy. This motion, made by Angela
Meyer and seconded by Aaron Whitwer, passed.
Motion to approve the updated forms and policies: 2005, 3001, 3003, 3003.1, 3004.1, 3042,
3043, 3058, 5063, & 6036. This motion, made by Betsy Frerichs and seconded by Jim Zvolanek,
— VII.G. Policy Review Policy 5001: Compulsory Attendance and Excess Absenteeism &
Dr. Prososki noted that school board must annually review its bullying and attendance policy and
no public hearings are required. In addition, each year the building principals are required to
work with the county attorney to review this policy as well.
VII.H. Declare the Old Kick Wheel as Surplus for Immediate Sale or Disposal
Motion to declare the old kick wheel as surplus for immediate sale or disposal. This motion,
made by Jim Zvolanek and seconded by Betsy Frerichs, passed.
The district plans to advertise the kick wheel for $600, as per the recommendation from Mrs.
Motion to adjourn the meeting at 9:10 a.m. This motion, made by Dana Dorn and seconded by
The next Regular Board meeting is scheduled for 7:30 p.m., July 12, 2021, at Southern Jr./Sr.
High School Auditorium in Wymore. The Board of Education will usually adhere to the
sequence of the published agenda, but reserves the right to adjust the order of items if necessary
and may elect to amend the agenda as deemed necessary.
